headline: 1MDB Final Recovery Reaches Only 38% of RM42B Losses
context: The 1MDB task force reported cumulative asset recovery of RM16.1 billion by end-2025, representing 38% of the estimated RM42 billion in total losses, with remaining recoverable assets scattered across 8 jurisdictions.

cards.big: RM25.9 billion still missing from 1MDB — after 10 years, Malaysia has recovered barely a third of what was stolen
cards.sub: Remaining assets locked in Swiss, Singapore, and US legal proceedings may take another decade to resolve

cards.big: 1MDB debt servicing alone has cost RM8.3 billion since 2015 — interest payments on money that was stolen
cards.sub: Annual RM1.7 billion debt service diverts funds equivalent to building 340 rural schools per year

cards.big: Goldman Sachs settlement of RM16 billion was largest but bank's 1MDB bond fees of RM2.4 billion were pure profit on fraud
cards.sub: No Goldman Sachs executive served prison time despite DOJ finding the bank 'knowingly participated' in scheme

cards.big: 1MDB task force staffing reduced from 48 to 18 officers since 2022 — recovery momentum clearly declining
cards.sub: Cross-border asset recovery requires sustained diplomatic pressure that election cycles consistently interrupt

cards.big: 1MDB isn't just a financial scandal — it's RM42 billion in schools, hospitals, and roads that Malaysians will never have
Like recovering 38 cents from every dollar stolen — better than nothing, but you still cannot build the hospital that dollar was meant for
cards.sub: Partial recovery cannot undo the opportunity cost of a decade of stolen public investment

cards.big: Should Malaysia establish a permanent asset recovery unit with 10-year funding to pursue remaining RM25.9 billion?
